summary: Finished matching Samus's ftSs_SpecialLw_1 translation, removing the 671-line assembly fallback and promoting src/melee/ft/chara/ftSamus/ftSs_SpecialLw_1.c to a Matching object. The C-side changes were small but targeted: helper inlining/static qualifiers, GET_FIGHTER usage, selected casts to Fighter_GObj, and removal of obsolete ftCollisionBox casts for Samus height_attributes calls.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/ft/chara/ftSamus/ftSs_SpecialLw_1.s, removing the remaining assembly implementation for this Samus special-low file.
Changed configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftSamus/ftSs_SpecialLw_1.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Changed obj_files.mk to link src/melee/ft/chara/ftSamus/ftSs_SpecialLw_1.c.o instead of the asm ftSs_SpecialLw_1.s.o fallback.
Reworked checkStateVar1 from a non-static inline using gobj->user_data into an inline static helper using GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Introduced inline static setSamusBits(Fighter* fp, int val) to assign fp->mv.ss.unk6.x0 in a shape that matches the original code.
Adjusted ftSs_SpecialLwBomb_Anim and ftSs_SpecialAirLwBomb_Anim to use GET_FIGHTER where needed and to cast selected calls to Fighter_GObj.
Removed old TODO casts to ftCollisionBox when passing &samus_attr->height_attributes to ft_80082888 and ft_800824A0.
smart_moves: The PR did not rewrite the whole file; it made minimal C-shape changes needed to retire the assembly object.
Factoring the bitfield assignment through inline static setSamusBits likely helped produce the required store/bitfield code shape without changing observed behavior.
Switching from direct gobj->user_data access to GET_FIGHTER(gobj) aligned this file with common fighter-source conventions and likely improved matching codegen.
Keeping casts only where needed, such as Fighter_GObj casts on ftColl_8007B0C0, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, and ft_8008A2BC call sites, avoided broader type churn.
The build-system promotion was complete: both configure.py and obj_files.mk were updated, and the old assembly file was deleted.
decomp_lessons: For final asm removal, verify both the object classification and the object-file list: configure.py must mark the C file Matching and obj_files.mk must stop referencing the asm .s.o.
Tiny helper functions can be useful matching tools, especially around bitfield or packed-member assignments such as fp->mv.ss.unk6.x0.
GET_FIGHTER(gobj) may produce a more reusable and match-friendly pattern than manually reading gobj->user_data in fighter code.
When call prototypes and source types do not line up exactly, local casts can be enough to preserve matching without changing shared headers.
Remove stale TODO casts once the expression type matches the callee signature; here &samus_attr->height_attributes no longer needed an explicit ftCollisionBox cast in the diff.
naming_conventions: Samus special-low symbols use the ftSs_SpecialLw prefix.
Ground and aerial entry points are distinguished as ftSs_SpecialLw_Enter and ftSs_SpecialAirLw_Enter.
Bomb-state callbacks follow the action callback suffix convention: ftSs_SpecialLwBomb_Anim, ftSs_SpecialAirLwBomb_Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.

Unidentified internal helpers retain address-suffixed names such as ftSs_SpecialLw_8012ADF0, ftSs_SpecialLw_8012AEBC, ftSs_SpecialLw_8012AF38, ftSs_SpecialLw_8012B570, ftSs_SpecialLw_8012B5F0, and ftSs_SpecialLw_8012B668.
Local matching helpers in this PR used inline static and lowerCamelCase names: setSamusBits and checkStateVar1.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the full assembly fallback only after the C source matched.
Used inline static helpers to control generated code shape for state/bitfield updates.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) instead of gobj->user_data to influence both style and likely code generation.
Applied explicit Fighter_GObj casts at selected animation/collision call sites to satisfy prototypes without larger refactors.
Preserved behavior around cmd_vars[0], cmd_vars[1], mv.ss.unk6.x0, and mv.ss.unk6.x4 while changing only expression shape and typing.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter files where bitfield assignment helpers are used to match codegen.
Search for remaining ftSamus SpecialLw TODO casts or height_attributes casts.
Check nearby Samus special files for direct gobj->user_data access that might need GET_FIGHTER for matching.
Find other PRs that remove asm files by changing both configure.py and obj_files.mk.
